reviews.title: Acceptable
reviews.text:
Spend most of my business trip at reception,  I booked two traditional standard corner rooms, both rooms were filthy  after paying over $3,350 for 4 nights. I complained and I was asked to go back into the room and show and watch the cleaner clean it.  Having just arrived from a 12 hour flight at 11: pm.
The cleaner did apologies and told me the room had not be cleaned and was missing towels etc.
She could have spend a year cleaning the room and that would not have made a difference. it was full a grime, with masking tape which was half ripped off on the edges of the horrid carpet. This hotel should not have a sign of the name Westin on its door. This was more like a low end motel. I would not even give it a two star rating. The door men were the only welcoming part of the trip.

reviews.title: Not a 4.5 level hotel
reviews.text:
If you're expecting 4.5-star hotel service, this property may not meet your expectations. The front desk staff were noticeably indifferent, which made us feel unwelcome from the start. We experienced issues with the Wi-Fi, and unfortunately, the staff did not assist in resolving them.
Surprisingly, there was no refrigerator in the room. If you need one, there's an additional $20 charge—something I wouldn’t expect from a hotel that positions itself at the Westin level. While the location is convenient for public transportation, it tends to be overcrowded and may not suit those seeking a quiet environment.
Overall, we were disappointed and will not be staying at this hotel again

The waiting area near check in desk was well designed and nice to sit there before/after checking in. 

The area is extremely noisey, due to the bad NYC traffic. At any time any point you will hear the honking and ambulance got stuck due to the bad traffic. Just imagin the noises 24/7....

reviews.title: Conveniently located around Times Square
reviews.text:
The hotel in essence is not bad if one prefers a non personal approach. Its a big chain hotel with professional but not friendly staff. Great if its the first time visiting NYC as its very conveniently located. 

Pros:

Conveniently located in Times Square
Food in the restaurant is not too bad, kitchen open until 22:30 so its great if you need to eat after watching a Broadway show.

Food, shopping and Broadway steps away from the Hotel too.


Cons:

Very busy hotel, lots of  guests coming in and out
Not very attentive or personal. 
Very noisy as the street below is always busy with cars, sirens and people
Can smell cannibis almost immediately as you step out of the hotel
Carpet in the room looks a bit dirty
Restaurant needs more staff.

reviews.text:
Although Times Square is super busy, there's no more central location in New York. This is a very convenient place to stay.

The hotel has strong assets - new building, reasonably sized rooms, great Westin beds. Staff was reasonably friendly, too.

A little extra attention would move this hotel from satisfactory/good to very good:
- curtains that are really opaque
- less boring room decor
- real milk for the in-room coffee, not tiny packets of powered non-dairy "creamer"
- and of course more reasonable prices.

reviews.title: Worst hotel experience ever
reviews.positives: The only thing I liked is the amazing support from John the porter. He's a gem of a guy and is the only reason I'm writing this review because if it wasn't for him, I would have walked out of the hotel and not stayed there. His professional attitude and approach and how he calmed me down was much better than the front desk staff and their manager.
reviews.negatives: Everything other than John. I arrived at the hotel at 5:30pm and my room wasn't ready. I was told it would be ready in an hour. I needed to do some shopping anyway, even though I was jet lagged, I was fine and went out with the promise that they were going to call me when my room was ready. I returned to the hotel at 8pm, no one had called and my room was still not ready. I complained and was offered a $50 credit at their bar but all I wanted was a room to rest. 45 minutes later I was finally given a room... I was charged for the full fare, offered 15k Marriott points for the inconvenience that continued at the breakfast with the waiter insisting on seeing the voucher which I didn't know I was given until he showed me what it looked like, then charged me for the breakfast until I got to my room and brought him back the voucher and didn't believe me when I showed him the booking. What hotel in the world doesn't have a computer system to show which room has breakfast included or not and has to rely on vouchers?!?! All in all, this was by far the worst experience I've ever had at a Marriott chain hotel and especially the Westin that's supposed to be a luxury brand. They were far from it... Except for John the hero.

reviews.title: Way under our expactations
reviews.positives:
Quick Check-In: Room was ready by 2 PM and check-in was smooth.

Friendly Staff: Helpful and courteous team, especially at the front desk.

Lobby & Elevators: Modern, state-of-the-art, and efficient even with 45 floors—elevators were fast and never kept guests waiting long.

Deluze Room Partial Upgrade: Upgraded Deluxe room had a newly renovated bathroom and updated curtains.
reviews.negatives:
Outdated Rooms: Initial room and even the upgraded one (apart from bathroom and curtains) felt old and worn.

Poor In-Room Amenities: Missing essentials like:

Slippers

Shower cap

Vanity kit (cotton buds, pads, nail file)

Dental kit (toothbrush & toothpaste)

Comb or hairbrush

Sanitary bags

Inconsistent Water Supply: The promised two bottles of water per day were not always provided.

Hidden/Deceptive Fees: $35 destination fee described misleadingly by management as a government tax.

Limited Concierge Services: Only one concierge for over 900 rooms led to long, frustrating queues to redeem attraction tickets.

Frustrating Ticket Process: Guests had to queue daily for tickets, leading many to give up due to the inconvenience.

reviews.title: Heart of the City
reviews.text: My mother and I had a good time staying at Westin Times Sq. primarily because of the location. It is located between 43rd St and 8th Avenue, so it is not quite smack dab in the middle of the loud noise along Broadway. There is a nice deli across from the hotel, and a few steps up the street there is a quaint little pizzeria that serves really good food at reasonable prices. It is called Bella Vita. Highly recommended. The hotel is also very nice and the room is very comfortable. But the wifi rate is quite expensive, $19.95 per night for high speed. And there isn't much of a view from the regular rooms. Once, the housekeeping skipped our room even  after we called to make sure they know we are leaving the room after noon time. So after a long day we came back to unmade beds and full waste baskets. We had to call the front desk twice to get the housekeeping staff service our room at 9 p.m. which is kind of annoying because we were already very tired. I also ordered a package from Amazon, which arrived a bit later than expected, but the FedEx staff seemed annoyed when they had to help me, and the front desk staff didn't seem to care. In short, if the package isn't there they aren't going to help you track it, either. Which is fair, and fine, but still rather disappointing. Other than that, we had a pleasant stay and everything else was good and proper.

reviews.title: Dated property at a good location
reviews.text:
The location of the hotel is really good if you don’t mind staying in Times Square which is a bit chaotic. 

We are a group of three girls, and we find the room to be spacious and comfortable for us.

However, we would appreciate it if the hotel could at least do some minor upgrades to the rooms such as curtains, shower curtains, lighting etc. painting over stains on the walls will help a lot, we spotted a few brown stains on our room’s ceiling which we thought were bugs!

On cleanliness- on our first night there, we found a bunch of hair on the wall of the shower that was not ours. That was quite off putting and the tiles in the toilet is black so didn’t really help… 

The staff of the hotel were excellent and housekeeping gave us new bottles of water every day which we really appreciated.

Overall - it felt like a hotel that had not been updated since the 90s. It was a good hotel for what we needed but I wouldn’t stay there if I wanted something more comfortable.
reviews.room_type: Traditional Double, Guest room, 2 Double, City view

reviews.title: Great location and team
reviews.text: The location is excellent, just a short walk from the subway and the staff were incredibly accommodating. Although check-in was officially at 4PM, they let us check in as early as 7AM and even gave us a free room upgrade. Do be prepared for some noise in the early morning and late at night, as it’s right in the heart of Times Square, but that was expected. Being a large hotel, it can get quite busy, especially with conferences and large groups, like during our stay.

reviews.title: 冷蔵庫は使えません
reviews.text:
物価の高いアメリカNYで立地の割に安く、価格面では大変満足でした。
が、中でも割安な旧タイプの部屋では冷蔵庫に鍵がかかっており利用に1日20ドルと言われ、冷たい飲み物を冷やすのを諦めました。夏の旅行では、その点が非常に残念でした。
また、5泊しましたがシーツ交換はありませんでした。よくある交換希望カードも見当たらず、1度は交換して欲しかったと思いました。
ただ、最初に戻りますが立地の割に安価なのは良いと思います。旧タイプの部屋は高級ホテルというよりはビジネスホテルと思った方が良いです。

reviews.title: 差
reviews.text: 本来刚到入住的时候很高兴因为就在时代广场上位置非常好。可吃早餐的时候真的是让人没有想到，在agoda上订的明明是包含早餐的，入住的时候前台也说可以吃早餐，但是我们吃完早餐的时候服务员竟然拿来账单要收我们一人9美元，说我们的早餐卷只能吃冷的食物，所有热的食物都不能吃，吃了就要收费！然而没有一个人告诉我们我们不能吃热的食物！包括agoda订房间的时候也没写！这点agoda做的太差！前台也没告诉！结果理论了半天还是要收9美元一人，说这是规定，住了好几个国家的威斯汀酒店第一次碰到早餐还冷热分开收费的酒店，包括在美国别的城市住威斯汀也没碰到这种情况！反正我已经投诉了！大家定的时候长个心眼，或者入住时一定要问清楚前台！免得被坑，垃圾威斯汀

I wish this place had more professional staff, but unfortunately Elez did not deliver the level of service we expected. A friend called us on FaceTime, and while we were talking, Elez claimed we were "too loud." Instead of approaching us privately and respectfully, he chose to address it publicly, which was unnecessary and unprofessional.
It's Thanksgiving Day, and it certainly felt like Elez was unhappy to be working and took it out on us. Someone in a customer-facing role should know how to handle situations calmly and professionally. Based on this experience, we won't be returning. Restaurant Review

We stayed at the Westin and absolutely loved the hotel and the staff—they were wonderful. Unfortunately, our experience at the onsite restaurant for breakfast was disappointing.

Before heading home, three of us ate breakfast and had one bowl of oats,one bowl of cereal, and a small dumpling-style item. The total came to $124 before tip. There were no posted prices for the buffet, and very little food was available at the time we visited. We likely would have eaten more, but there simply wasn’t much out.

For breakfast, the cost felt extremely high, especially given the limited selection. To put it in perspective, we later ate at Sarabeth’s and our bill was significantly less.

While the hotel itself was excellent, the breakfast pricing and lack of transparency were surprising and did not match the overall quality of our stay.

reviews.title: Excellent stay with kids. Perfect location and comfortable Hotel
reviews.positives:
Liked? Wow, a lot of things.
- Location (literally in the middle of the Theatre District) and very close to Subway stations.
- Comfortable, with an excellent king bed (which I shared with my two small kids).
- Good bed linens (my kids loved... it was hard to wake them up).
- Perfect view from my room on the 43th floor (I could see the Empire States, the Times Square, Brooklin Bridge etc).
- From my floor I could hear sirens, so I can say my suite was very quiet (but listening the city noises is also a NY experience).
- Good natural lightning (lots of windows). And good curtains to keep the room real dark in the morning.
- Excellent shower, with blindex, good water pressure and also with a nice view! Loved the shampoo and body wash.
- Good elevators with good waiting times.
That's it. Recommend it!
reviews.negatives: It's not fare to say I didn't like something... but I missed a mini fridge in my room. And I would also like having some bigger towels. But these are details. I wouldn't consider these negative. Maybe, if I requested, they could have helped me.

reviews.positives:
We had a great room on the 43 floor where we got a  view of the Empire State building. 

They give you $35 toward food for each day but their prices are high and would easily go over that amount. However we talked with some of the security guards and they recommended a place for breakfast that was a short walk and it was great food. We really appreciated their help and kindness.
reviews.negatives: We got to the hotel about 9:00PM as our flight from Italy didn't get in until that evening. Now first I want to say that the young lady at the desk was very helpful and professional. We informed her that we were to have a roll-a-way bed and she said that it would be right up. By 10:00 it still had not arrived and we called down and were informed they would get it sent up right away. We were all so tired that we did not call again so one of us ended up making the bathtub a bed. In the morning we notified the manager of what happened and he took care of the matter right a way. We got the roll-a-way now at no extra charge and got some taken off our bill. Not sure what the breakdown was in communications but I was pleased in how they handled it.

reviews.positives: We were so impressed with the employees specially the one who helped us from the start,Mary Jane.We were so tired coming from a 10 day cruise from Brooklyn terminal and waited almost an hour getting an XL Uber ride.  Mary Jane granted our request to have an early check in so we were beyond happy when she told us there was a room with the specification we requested  that was available. Going home to California, She also helped us  by getting a taxi with a flat rate of $85 from Time Square to JFK airport otherwise we would have waited for another Uber ride that would have charged us 20% more. She  was indeed a great blessings to us (2 senior citizens)  and all the customers who needed assistance when their need is so great.  This is the third time I stayed at this Westin and it will definitely not be the last. Location is perfect and employees are awesome!!!!!
